claimText: Biostimulants function by stimulating natural processes to benefit crop quality, independently of their nutrient content.
evidenceExcerpt: Biostimulants stimulate natural processes to benefit crop quality independently of their nutrient content.
examUse: Statement-based question on the primary function of biostimulants.
distractorHooks: Claiming biostimulants primarily function by providing essential macronutrients to the soil.
Stating that biostimulants are dependent on their nutrient content to improve crop quality.

claimText: Biostimulants differ from fertilizers as they do not supply nutrients directly, and they differ from pesticides as they do not control pests.
evidenceExcerpt: They do not supply nutrients directly (unlike fertilizers) and do not control pests (unlike pesticides).
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing biostimulants from fertilizers and pesticides.
distractorHooks: Asserting that biostimulants are a sub-category of chemical pesticides.
Claiming biostimulants act as direct nutrient suppliers similar to NPK fertilizers.

claimText: In India, biostimulants are regulated under the Fertiliser (Inorganic, Organic or Mixed) (Control) Order, 1985.
evidenceExcerpt: They are regulated under the Fertiliser (Inorganic, Organic or Mixed) (Control) Order, 1985.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal and regulatory framework governing agricultural inputs.

claimText: Humic acids, seaweed extracts, liquid manure composting, and beneficial bacteria and fungi are examples of biostimulants.
evidenceExcerpt: Examples include humic acids, seaweed extracts, liquid manure composting, and beneficial bacteria and fungi.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying correct examples of biostimulants from a given list.
distractorHooks: Including synthetic urea as a biostimulant.
Including chemical herbicides or fungicides as biostimulant examples.
